properties.notes: Gaol is older English for Jail. | Winnipeg Courthouse at 494 Main St, built 1873 (served as Manitoba Legislature briefly). Sold to John Clements 1881; became saloon/variety theatre. Demolished 1884 for West Clements Block. DATECHECK: Born: 1873, Died: 1884

properties.notes: J.H. Ashdown Hardware brick building from 1875, expanded 1880. Destroyed by fire October 12, 1904 (sparked from Bulman Block across Bannatyne). Immediately rebuilt. DATECHECK: Born: 1875, Died: 1904-10-12

Fire Hall
properties.notes: Winnipeg first volunteer fire dept formed Sept 24 1874. First central fire hall at William Ave/Charlotte St. Full paid fire dept established 1882 with new equipment/hall. DATECHECK: Born: 1874-09-24, Died: ~1882

properties.name: Fire Hall
properties.notes: Early Winnipeg fire hall. First paid dept 1882. DATECHECK: Born: 1874-09-24, Died: ~1882

properties.name: City Hall
properties.notes: First Winnipeg City Hall, opened March 14 1876. Suffered chronic structural problems, had to be propped up. Demolished 1883 to make way for 2nd City Hall. Held Council Chamber, Registry Office, City Clerk's Office, and Police Office

properties.cause_of_death: Demo no later than 1889, make way for Manitoba Hotel

Grace Methodist Church
properties.notes: https://www.mhs.mb.ca/docs/sites/gracemethodist1.shtml | Grace Methodist Church (Main St/Water Ave). Opened Sept 17 1871. Two wings added east side in 1877. Insufficient space again by late 1880, building abandoned. Congregation at Notre Dame from 1883. DATECHECK: Born: 1871-09-17 (not 1877), Died: ~1880-12 (not 1889)
